brands.name: Molly Maid
brands.notes: Residential cleaning franchise founded 1979, part of the Neighborly (KKR-owned) platform; low franchise fee plus per-household territory fee, strong recurring-revenue model (91% of cleanings from recurring customers per 2025 FDD), but the US system has contracted for three straight years.

riskFlags.title: Three consecutive years of net US unit decline
riskFlags.detail: Per FDD data, US franchised units fell from 501 (start 2022) to 448 (end 2024): -20 in 2022, -17 in 2023, -16 in 2024. No company-owned locations.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter review of 2025 Molly Maid FDD

riskFlags.title: Franchisee complaints about Neighborly transfer/resale and non-compete practices
riskFlags.detail: Franchise-owner reviews of parent Neighborly on Glassdoor describe the franchisor as 'very litigious,' cite a 2-year post-term non-compete covering the territory plus a 25-mile radius, and allege Neighborly has little incentive to facilitate franchise resales (preferring territories go vacant for resale by Neighborly).

riskFlags.title: Parent Neighborly is private-equity owned (KKR)
riskFlags.detail: Neighborly, the parent platform, was acquired by private equity firm KKR from Harvest Partners in 2021. PE ownership can drive fee/system changes and eventual resale of the franchisor.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Franchisor requires at least $60,000 in readily available funds; Entrepreneur lists a $50,000 cash requirement

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2025 FDD Item 7 range $139,900-$197,200; franchisor website currently shows $144,150-$203,950

totalInvestmentMax.note: Upper bound of 2025 FDD range; franchisor site shows up to $203,950

franchiseFee.note: Plus a territory fee of $1 per Target Household (typically $45,000-$70,000 for a standard market)

royaltyPercent.note: Tiered license fee of 3% to 6.5% of gross sales, declining as gross sales scale

adFundPercent.note: 2% of gross sales; separate local marketing requirement of $1 per Target Household per year also applies

totalUnits.note: US franchised units as of Dec 31, 2024 per 2025 FDD; zero company-owned. Entrepreneur lists 444 current units

closureCount.note: Net US unit decline during 2024 (464 to 448) per FDD; third consecutive year of net decline

employeesRequired.note: Number of employees required to run, per Entrepreneur listing

item19Available.note: 2025 FDD Item 19 reports gross sales per cleaning ($173.43 avg) and per target household ($16.23 avg) for 417 franchised businesses, not annual unit revenue — hence no avgUnitRevenue field

veteranDiscount.note: 20% off the initial franchise/territory fee via VetFran

multiUnitAvailable.note: 2025 FDD Item 19: 197 operators ran 417 franchised businesses, indicating widespread multi-territory ownership

exclusiveTerritories.note: Entrepreneur lists exclusive territories as 'No'; territories are defined by Target Household counts

isGrowing.note: US franchised unit count declined each year 2022-2024 per FDD data

brands.name: Mr. Handyman
brands.notes: Residential/commercial repair and maintenance franchise under Neighborly (KKR-owned); growing system (297 to 347 US units 2022-2024) with a strong FDD Item 19 ($763K average gross sales for single-unit owners in 2024), offset by a high franchise fee and large mandatory local marketing minimums.

riskFlags.title: High mandatory local marketing minimums
riskFlags.detail: Beyond the 2% brand fund, franchisees face a Minimum Local Marketing Spending Requirement of $60,000 in Year 1 and $75,000 in Year 2, then 8% of prior year's gross sales annually — a heavy fixed cost during ramp-up.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter review of 2025 Mr. Handyman FDD

riskFlags.title: Prior franchisee rescission litigation in Canada (disclosed in FDD)
riskFlags.detail: Mr. Handyman of Toronto West, Inc. v. Mr. Handyman of Canada Company (Ontario Superior Court, filed Dec 4, 2013): former franchisees alleged violations of Ontario's Arthur Wishart Act and sought rescission of their franchise agreement. Dated, but disclosed in FDD Item 3.

riskFlags.title: Franchisee complaints about Neighborly transfer/resale and non-compete practices
riskFlags.detail: Franchise-owner reviews of parent Neighborly on Glassdoor describe the franchisor as 'very litigious,' cite a 2-year post-term non-compete covering the territory plus a 25-mile radius, and allege Neighborly has little incentive to facilitate franchise resales (preferring territories go vacant for resale by Neighborly).

riskFlags.title: Parent Neighborly is private-equity owned (KKR)
riskFlags.detail: Neighborly, the parent platform, was acquired by private equity firm KKR from Harvest Partners in 2021. PE ownership can drive fee/system changes and eventual resale of the franchisor.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Franchisor requires at least $50,000 in readily available funds; some directories (FranNet) cite $65,000 liquid

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2025 FDD Item 7 range $143,150-$179,600; franchisor website currently shows $161,900-$215,000

totalInvestmentMax.note: Upper bound of 2025 FDD range; franchisor site shows up to $215,000

franchiseFee.note: Per 2025 FDD and Entrepreneur; an older figure of $59,900 appears in some directories

royaltyPercent.note: 7% of gross sales (3.5% on material/subcontractor revenue)

adFundPercent.note: Marketing/MAP fee of 2% of gross sales

avgUnitRevenue.note: 2025 FDD Item 19: average gross sales of single-unit franchisees for calendar year 2024; 2-unit franchisees averaged $1,144,897

totalUnits.note: US franchised units as of Dec 31, 2024 per 2025 FDD; all franchised, none company-owned. Entrepreneur lists 351 current units

yearFounded.note: Founded 1996 per FDD-based review; Entrepreneur's listing shows founded 2000 (year operations/franchising began)

item19Available.note: 2025 FDD Item 19 discloses average gross sales by ownership cohort

veteranDiscount.note: 15% off the franchise fee through the VetFran program

multiUnitAvailable.note: 2025 FDD Item 19 reports a 2-unit franchisee cohort, confirming multi-unit ownership

exclusiveTerritories.note: Entrepreneur lists exclusive territories as 'No'; Neighborly markets population-based territories

isGrowing.note: US units grew 297 (start 2022) to 347 (end 2024); Entrepreneur shows +10% growth

brands.name: Mr. Electric
brands.notes: Residential/commercial electrical services franchise under Neighborly (KKR-owned), franchising since 1994; growing US system (170 to 211 units 2022-2024) with FDD Item 19 average gross sales scaling from ~$522K in the smallest territories to ~$2.58M in the largest.

riskFlags.title: Franchisee complaints about Neighborly transfer/resale and non-compete practices
riskFlags.detail: Franchise-owner reviews of parent Neighborly on Glassdoor describe the franchisor as 'very litigious,' cite a 2-year post-term non-compete covering the territory plus a 25-mile radius, and allege Neighborly has little incentive to facilitate franchise resales (preferring territories go vacant for resale by Neighborly).

riskFlags.title: Parent Neighborly is private-equity owned (KKR)
riskFlags.detail: Neighborly, the parent platform, was acquired by private equity firm KKR from Harvest Partners in 2021. PE ownership can drive fee/system changes and eventual resale of the franchisor.

riskFlags.title: Wide revenue dispersion by territory size
riskFlags.detail: 2025 FDD Item 19 shows average gross sales of $521,544 for the smallest territories (74K-300K population, 76 of 152 reporting units) versus $2,579,723 for the largest (1M-5M population) — half the system operates in the lowest-revenue segment, so territory selection materially drives outcomes.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter review of 2025 Mr. Electric FDD

liquidCapitalMin.note: Franchisor requires at least $65,000 in readily available funds

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2025 FDD range $152,000 (excl. real estate) to $314,925 plus any additional franchise fee; franchisor website currently shows $159,500-$357,425

totalInvestmentMax.note: Upper bound of 2025 FDD range; franchisor site shows up to $357,425

franchiseFee.note: For a territory of up to 100,000 population, plus $425 per additional 1,000 population

royaltyPercent.note: 6% of gross sales, with minimum royalty fees applied

avgUnitRevenue.note: Weighted average computed from 2025 FDD Item 19 segment averages (152 franchises reporting full-year 2024 sales): $521,544 avg for 74K-300K-population territories (76 units), $1,103,241 (25), $1,713,316 (30), $2,579,723 for 1M-5M territories (21)

totalUnits.note: US franchised units as of Dec 31, 2024 per 2025 FDD (all franchised); Entrepreneur lists 238 current units including international

item19Available.note: 2025 FDD Item 19 discloses gross sales by territory-population segment

veteranDiscount.note: 20% off the initial franchise fee through the VetFran program

multiUnitAvailable.note: Listing states both single-unit and multi-unit options are available

exclusiveTerritories.note: Entrepreneur lists exclusive territories as 'No'; franchisor marketing describes a 'protected, population-based territory' (min 100,000 residents)

isGrowing.note: US units grew 170 (start 2022) to 211 (end 2024), +22 net in 2024 alone

brands.name: Two Men and a Truck
brands.notes: North America's largest franchised moving company (founded 1985, franchising since 1989), owned by Roark Capital-backed ServiceMaster Brands since 2021; growing US system (284 to 338 franchised units 2022-2024) with very strong Item 19 economics ($2.37M average gross sales for full-year Metro single franchises in 2024) but a wide, capital-intensive investment range.

riskFlags.title: Acquired by Roark Capital-backed ServiceMaster Brands (2021)
riskFlags.detail: ServiceMaster Brands (owned by PE firm Roark Capital) acquired TWO MEN AND A TRUCK/International, Inc. on Aug 3, 2021 — its second acquisition since Roark bought ServiceMaster Brands in 2020. PE platform ownership can drive fee and system changes.

riskFlags.title: Escalating minimum royalties and layered tech/ad fees
riskFlags.detail: The 6% royalty carries minimum royalty amounts that escalate by year, plus a 1%-2% technology/support fee (minimum $1,200/month for Metro) and digital advertising charged at 15% of media spend on top of the 1% ad fund — fixed obligations that bite if revenue ramps slowly.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter review of 2025 Two Men and a Truck FDD

riskFlags.title: Michigan-only dispute venue in franchise agreement
riskFlags.detail: Older franchise disclosure summaries note the franchise agreement and area development agreement require franchisees to resolve disputes with the franchisor by litigation in Michigan, which may pressure out-of-state franchisees toward less favorable settlements. Verify the venue clause in the current FDD.
riskFlags.source: FreeFranchiseDocs Two Men and a Truck UFOC summary

nationwide.source: Two Men and a Truck franchise development site
nationwide.note: Franchisor's 'Franchise Markets' page lists standard franchise opportunities across states from Alabama to Wyoming and states the company 'continues to expand with available territories still open across the U.S.' (380+ locations in 47 states). Note: the Franchise Gator lead-gen listing currently accepts inquiries from only 15 states, likely reflecting ad targeting rather than offering scope.

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2025 FDD range $146,950-$506,450 for standard (Metro) franchises; Entrepreneur shows $92,100-$506,450 including the smaller Mod Market model

franchiseFee.note: Metro Market franchise fee ranges $50,000-$165,000 by territory; Mod Market (small-market) fee is $30,000-$40,000 — lower bound of standard Metro range used

royaltyPercent.note: 6% of gross sales with escalating minimum royalties; separate 1%-2% technology/support fee applies

adFundPercent.note: 1% of gross sales, plus digital advertising billed at 15% of media spend

avgUnitRevenue.note: 2025 FDD Item 19: average annual gross sales of the 151 Metro Market single franchises operating the full 2024 calendar year; multi-unit franchisees averaged $4,406,768; Mod Market Year-1 units averaged $484,134

totalUnits.note: US franchised units as of Dec 31, 2024 per 2025 FDD; Entrepreneur lists 385 system-wide including Canada, Ireland and UK

item19Available.note: 2025 FDD Item 19 discloses gross sales for single, multi-unit and Mod Market cohorts

veteranDiscount.note: 20% off the franchise fee for veterans

isGrowing.note: US franchised units grew 284 (start 2022) to 338 (end 2024); Entrepreneur shows +18.1% growth

brands.name: Merry Maids
brands.notes: One of the largest US residential cleaning franchises (founded 1979, franchising since 1980), owned by Roark Capital-backed ServiceMaster Brands; modest entry cost and a disclosed Item 19 ($487K average gross sales in 2024), but the US system has shed units at an accelerating rate for three straight years.

riskFlags.title: Accelerating US unit contraction (-187 units over 3 years)
riskFlags.detail: Per FDD data, US franchised units fell from 989 (start 2022) to 802 (end 2024): -43 in 2022, -62 in 2023, -82 in 2024 — roughly 19% system shrinkage with the pace of decline accelerating each year. Zero company-owned units.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter review of 2025 Merry Maids FDD

riskFlags.title: Parent ServiceMaster Brands is private-equity owned (Roark Capital)
riskFlags.detail: Roark Capital acquired ServiceMaster Brands — including Merry Maids — for $1.5 billion in 2020. PE platform ownership can drive fee/system changes and eventual resale of the franchisor.

riskFlags.title: High monthly technology fee
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur's listing shows a $499/month technology fee, on the high side for a home-based-style residential cleaning system, on top of the 7% royalty and 1.3% ad fund.

nationwide.source: Franchise Gator Merry Maids listing (franchisor-fed directory)
nationwide.note: Listing states 'Merry Maids is currently accepting inquiries from the following states:' and enumerates all 50 states plus the District of Columbia; the franchisor's own site also maintains an 'Available Markets' territories page (franchise.merrymaids.com/franchise-territories).

liquidCapitalMin.note: Entrepreneur cash requirement; franchisor materials also cite about $50,000 cash needed

franchiseFee.note: Per 2025 FDD; some directories list $37,500-$51,500 varying by territory size

royaltyPercent.note: 7% of gross sales, reducible to 6% or 5% based on calendar-year gross sales incentives

avgUnitRevenue.note: 2025 FDD Item 19: average gross sales (all billings, collected or not) of 306 qualified franchises active throughout calendar 2024; top 10% averaged $1,159,708, bottom 10% $115,387

totalUnits.note: US franchised units as of Dec 31, 2024 per 2025 FDD; Entrepreneur lists 1,556 units including international

closureCount.note: Net US unit decline during 2024 (884 to 802) per FDD; -43 in 2022 and -62 in 2023

fddAvailable.note: 2025 FDD (271 pages, issued August 2025) publicly reviewed

item19Available.note: 2025 FDD Item 19 discloses average gross sales with top/bottom decile breakdowns

veteranDiscount.note: 20% off the franchise fee for veterans; some directories also note 10% off for minorities and women

isGrowing.note: US franchised units declined three consecutive years (989 to 802, 2022-2024); Entrepreneur shows -4% growth
